what_it_does: A git diff patch to runtime/suite.py that extends the focus-handling logic of the Suite class to recognize a new `mockup://<file>` address in the same focus.selected list as ui:// addresses. When present, it path-safely loads the referenced static HTML mockup from design/mockups/, caps its size at 14000 bytes (with a fail-loud truncation marker), and injects its raw content into the turn's live-state context block so the right-hand-man can read the design mockup for the operator and explain it.
observations: Adds parsing of mockup:// entries from raw_selected, filtered out of the node-id `selected` list
Validates the filename with a regex requiring a bare <name>.html basename (no paths)
Resolves the repo root via os.path.dirname twice on __file__ and constructs design/mockups/<name>
Uses os.path.realpath + os.path.commonpath containment check to prevent path traversal outside design/mockups/
Three honest failure messages injected into ctx: refused (bad name), cannot see (no file), or the content block
Reads file with errors='replace' and caps at CAP=14000 chars, appending an explicit truncation notice
Injects a long natural-language instruction telling the model this is a static HTML design artifact (not the live app) and to explain it at plain-language altitude for a non-code-reading operator
mockup:// check happens BEFORE the node-id filter so it isn't swallowed as a non-existent node
standouts: Comments cite 'seams-rhm Seam 4' and frame this as WIDENING the existing focus vocabulary, 'never a new mechanism'
Comment notes it mirrors the bridge's _safe_mockup_path for path safety
Comment cites an advisor decision to inject RAW HTML rather than pre-build an extraction pipeline unless a test shows truncation/junk
Explicitly invokes the grounding rule: a filename alone would correctly draw 'I can't see that'
conventions: Honest/fail-loud surfacing — never silently drop or feed partial content as whole (cites 'rule 4')
Path-safety via realpath + commonpath containment, mirroring an existing helper
ui://-style address vocabulary extended rather than replaced
Dense rationale-bearing inline comments with provenance, consistent with repo discipline
concerns: import re aliased as _re is done inside the loop block (functional but unusual placement)
notes: A patch teaching the Suite turn-builder to load a static mockup HTML into live-state when the operator selects a mockup:// focus, so the right-hand-man can read the design for a non-code operator.
questions: What is the bridge's _safe_mockup_path that this mirrors, and where is it defined?
Where does the mockup:// value originate in the frontend focus payload?
Is 14000 chars sufficient for typical mockups, or will truncation be common?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (patch; the diff implements exactly the mockup-grounding behavior its comments describe)
apparent_themes: Grounding: the model may only speak about content present in the live-state block
Operator does not read code — the right-hand-man reads artifacts for them
Decision provenance encoded in comments (advisor, seam references)
intention_signals: Trying to let the operator point at a developer-authored design mockup and have the assistant explain it in plain language, by reusing the focus.selected channel and the grounding-block contract rather than building a new review pipeline.
novelty: The mockup:// focus address that rides the existing ui:// selected list and injects raw static HTML into the grounded live-state so a non-code operator can be walked through a design they cannot read themselves.
contribution: Contributes the server-side mockup:// resolution: parsing, path-safe loading from design/mockups/, size-capping, honest failure messaging, and the grounded explain-this-mockup context injection.
summary_for_embedding: Patch to runtime/suite.py adding mockup:// focus handling that path-safely loads a static design mockup's HTML into the grounded live-state block.
intention_for_embedding: To let the right-hand-man read a developer-authored design mockup for a non-code operator and explain what they are looking at.
